lines.speaker: Ivy Sophista
lines.text: What are you waiting for? Thaerisk is waiting for you upstairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Ivy Sophista
lines.text: Upstairs. Now!

lines.speaker: Ivy Sophista
lines.text: Good, you're here. I take it you received Idria's message?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Message?

lines.speaker: Ivy Sophista
lines.text: It was not delivered to you? This is concerning... Still, the point is you're here. Now, are you willing to help us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did, but I have a few questions...

lines.speaker: Ivy Sophista
lines.text: They will have to wait. There is only one question of importance at the moment: Are you willing to help us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so...

lines.speaker: Ivy Sophista
lines.text: Good. Meet Thaerisk upstairs. There is much to discuss and Lucien's spies are everywhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... Did you say Lucien?

lines.speaker: Ivy Sophista
lines.text: Upstairs. Now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll pass. This is all a bit weird for me.

lines.speaker: Ivy Sophista
lines.text: So be it. You know where to find me if you change your mind.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk before starting the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Ah, I'm just keeping an eye on things and making sure nothing untoward happens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's reassuring.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: One day I hope it will be.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Ah, you made it. Given recent events, that in itself is something for us to be thankful about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Unfortunately so. We've already lost several of our brethren in recent days. Lucien's agents are only growing bolder.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but I'm going to need some explanation. What's going on, and what does it have to do with Lucien?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: You mean you do not know? Idria's message should have explained the situation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I never received any message.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: I see. In that case, I will explain what I can, but I will need to be brief.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but I'm going to need a bit more of an explanation on what's going on. The message I received didn't have too many details.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Yes, of course. I will explain what I can, but I will need to be brief.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: My name is Thaerisk Cemphier and I lead a group of Guthixians called Crux Eqal. Our duty is a straightforward one: To safeguard this world from the chaos of imbalance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Safeguard? Doesn't that risk conflicting with the peaceful nature of being a druid?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Yes, it sadly does. However, our work is a necessity. Without those of us willing to do what must be done to protect this world, peace will never be more than a fantasy.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Now, while I do enjoy debating concepts such as these, we are getting sidetracked. Let's get back to the matter at hand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lucien?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Precisely. We have been keeping an eye on the Mahjarrat for some time. We consider many of them to be grave threats to the safety of our world.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: However, truth be told, until recently, we didn't consider Lucien to be among that number. All that changed when you came along.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: When you helped Lucien obtain the Staff of Armadyl, you set him on a path to unimaginable power. After all, Zamorak himself was once a Mahjarrat, until he used the Staff of Armadyl to attain godhood...

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: However, truth be told, until recently, we didn't consider Lucien to be among that number. All that changed when, despite your best attempts to stop him, he managed to obtain the Staff of Armadyl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He has the Staff?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: I'm afraid so, and with it, he is now on a path leading towards unimaginable power. After all, Zamorak himself was once a Mahjarrat, until he used the Staff of Armadyl to attain godhood...

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hang on a moment. I hear someone approaching from downstairs. Let us wait for them to pass.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Assassins!

lines.speaker: Assassin 1
lines.text: Now!

lines.speaker: Assassin 2
lines.text: I'm on it!

lines.speaker: Assassin 2
lines.text: Nothing personal, adventurer!

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk after killing the assassins

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again, Thaerisk.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Welcome back, [player name]. What news do you have?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing at the moment.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Remember, we need you to investigate a treasure hunter by the name of Movario. Given that he hired a bodyguard from the Khazard Army, I'd recommend starting at the Fight Arena.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thaerisk, are you okay?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Yes, I think so, but that was far too close. We have been dealing with the threat of Lucien's assassins for some time now, but that's the first time they've attacked us here in Taverley.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lucien must consider you to be quite the threat to him.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Perhaps he also sees you as a threat as well. You may have worked with him before, but I suspect that means little to someone like him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You think the assassins were targeting me?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Who could say for sure, but it may not be a coincidence that his assassins attacked so soon after your arrival.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Perhaps he also sees you as a threat as well. It may not be a coincidence that his assassins attacked so soon after your arrival.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Regardless, this is another reminder that time is not on our side. We must find out what Lucien is planning and stop him before it's too late.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: I realise that's easier said than done, but I believe we have what it takes to do so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What resources do you have?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: We're still on the back foot compared to Lucien, but that is changing fast. My order has already formed an alliance with the Guardians of Armadyl and we have reached out to the Temple Knights as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's quite the collective.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Indeed. Never would I have expected to see the followers of three different gods unite in this way, but our differences mean little compared to the threat Lucien poses.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you'd like me to join this alliance as well? Even though I helped Lucien before?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Yes. I am aware of many of your past feats. You have regularly fought against the evils of this world. I believe there is more to you than what happened with Lucien.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you'd like me to join this alliance as well?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Yes. You have opposed Lucien before, and you have shown that you have the skills needed to make a difference in this fight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. So what would you have me do?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Our spies have been trying to track Lucien for some time, but he is proving difficult to locate. However, he has been expanding his resources at a rapid rate. We might be able to take advantage of this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: He's been forming alliances with all manner of groups and individuals. Such a large collective is likely to have plenty of weak links for us to exploit.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: With that in mind, we'd like you to investigate a treasure hunter by the name of Movario. We believe he has been working with Lucien for some time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Movario? What can you tell me about him?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: He's magically trained, has adventured, and has spent a lot of time in various archives.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So he's a bit of a history buff?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Indeed. We suspect that his combined magical and historical knowledge is highly valuable to Lucien. In fact, we believe that it was Movario who first tracked down the Staff of Armadyl for him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas where I should start?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: According to our spies, Movario has hired a member of the Khazard Army called Darve to act as his bodyguard. As such, I'd recommend you begin at the Fight Arena.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything else you can tell me about Movario?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: He first became a person of interest after you stole the Staff of Armadyl. He was regularly sighted in the area around the Temple of Ikov in the days beforehand.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: He first became a person of interest after the Staff of Armadyl was stolen. He was regularly sighted in the area around the Temple of Ikov in the days beforehand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't seem like a lot to go on. How can you be sure he's involved?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Truth be told, we still don't have absolute confirmation that he is. However, few people have the skills needed to locate something like the Staff of Armadyl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Anything else of note?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: He seems to have been researching something new of late. He's been sighted in many libraries all across the world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's he researching?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: We're not sure. His focus seems to be on the God Wars and the gods themselves. Saradomin and Guthix in particular.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Given what happened with the Staff, our fear is that Lucien has him looking for more sources of godly power.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's... concerning. How did you find all of this out?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Most of this information actually came from a single spy of ours. She obtained it by talking to Reldo of all people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Reldo? A local librarian was the source of information on a master artefact hunter working with Lucien?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Yes, he's quite an interesting individual. Most of course know him as an accomplished scholar, but I've found that there's a lot more to him than first meets the eye...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do we have any more information on what Lucien is up to?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: At this point, I'm afraid not. From what we can tell, he's mostly operated alone until very recently. However, since acquiring the Staff of Armadyl, he's been forming quite the alliance of individuals.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: It is clear he's planning something. Something big. However, any attempts by our spies to locate him have resulted in violent ends at the hands of his new allies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are these new allies?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Right now they mostly seem to consist of various mercenaries and the like. However, given the resources he now has access to, we believe he's also allied with some larger organisations.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: On top of that, there's also evidence to suggest he's been building an undead army somewhere in the Wilderness. There has been an increase in sightings of zombies and skeletons in the area.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's no shortage of necromancers, especially in the Wilderness. How can you be sure it's Lucien?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Nothing is confirmed and this point, but these undead seem to be specifically targeting our spies in the area.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Sounds like we need to learn more as soon as we can.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: That is why I'm hoping that Movario will lead us to some answers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about Crux Eqal?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: We are a group who believe that too many of our fellow Guthixians have allowed themselves to become spellbound by the beauty of nature. The wider world is constantly at threat, but they no longer wish to see it.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Our organisation is still fairly new, and many of our members are on the younger side. However, we have the backing of many senior druids such as Sanfew and Kaqemeex.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hope you don't mind me saying that druids are not known to be the best fighters. How are you ensuring that you're prepared to take on these threats?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: You are not wrong, my friend. I have no doubt that every member of our order would willing fight to the last drop of blood if the situation required it, but it is not where our strength lies.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: However, I do not see that as a bad thing. The world has no shortage of warriors and heroes, and our kingdoms have armies capable of standing against many a danger. Instead, we focus on what they often lack.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: One example is our spy network. While still not quite as extensive as that of the Temple Knights, we have many who are experts in moving around unseen. After all, no one suspects a druid.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Then of course you have our Herblore skills, which are second to none. We are constantly making new advancements that let us supply our allies with brews and potions for all manner of purposes.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: We're already putting many of these potions to the test in our fight against Lucien.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Be careful out there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Spectator
lines.text: Hey, you're the one who beat Bouncer! Amazing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't happen to know anyone called Darve, do you?

lines.speaker: Spectator
lines.text: Can't say I do. Maybe try in the bar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: Hi, what can I get you? We have a range of quality brews.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anyone called Darve?

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: I might do. Why do you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm trying to find him.

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: Good for you, but that's not really got anything to do with me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well what about someone called Movario?

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: Movario? The mage?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you know him?

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: I'm aware of him. Couldn't tell you anything about him though, even if I wanted to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well do you know someone who can?

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: He sometimes uses the launderer just to the north of here. Maybe you should try talking to him. Now, would you like a drink?

sections.title: Talking to the Khazard Launderer again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. Could you remind me where I can get one of those broavs?

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: I think they came from the Feldip Hills. Apparently there's some hunting expert down there who knows how to catch them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perfect! Thanks for the help.

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: Those damn broavs! Now I'll need to start over again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Hello?

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: Oh, sorry, I didn't see you come in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is something wrong?

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: Just the usual sort of nonsense you get around here. One of the guards bought some broavs for tracking, but they keep getting distracted by my laundry and making a mess of it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... Anyway, you don't happen to know someone called Darve do you? Or maybe someone called Movario?

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: Movario? Oh yes I... Well, actually, I shouldn't really divulge client details. Times are tough enough as it is without me losing customers through gossiping!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would 500 coins change your mind?

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: Oh... well I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back shortly with the money.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now, what can you tell me about Movario?

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: Well he's a fairly regular customer of mine. In fact, I have some of his clothes here right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So does he live somewhere nearby?

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: I assume so, but I don't know where.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I need some way of tracking him down...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... What was it you were saying earlier about those broavs?

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: Broavs? Well one of the guards bought some for tracking, but they keep getting distracted by the laundry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't think I'd be able to use one to track Movario, do you?

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: I guess so, but they track using smell, so you'd need something that smells like him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like his dirty laundry, perhaps?

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: Oh, I see... I really shouldn't be giving out clothes belonging to customers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surely you have something he won't miss.

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: Well... I was thinking that one of his shirts wouldn't last the cleaning process. I suppose you can have it, but Movario can't find out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. So how would I go about getting myself one of those broavs?

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: I think they came from the Feldip Hills. Apparently there's some hunting expert down there who knows how to catch them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perfect! Thanks for all the help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can trust me.

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: Oh... yes, I suppose I can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! So what can you tell me about Movario?

lines.speaker: Khazard Launderer
lines.text: Well he's a fairly regular customer of mine. In fact, I have some of his clothes here right now.

sections.title: Talking to the hunting expert again

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Whoah there, stranger, careful where you're walking. I almost clobbered you there; thought you were a larupia or something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think you could help me with broavs?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Have you managed to catch one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: There's an old trapping spot to the west that should work perfectly. Remember, instead of luring the broav over your trap, you'll just want to bait it with a fungus from Mort Myre.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Whoah there, stranger, careful where you're walking. I almost clobbered you there; thought you were a larupia or something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think you could help me with broavs?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Ah, yes. You want one for some tracking I'm guessing? They're very useful for that sort of thing, but not easy to capture, especially not alive. Then, of course, there's the training.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Training? How do I train them?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: I only know of one person who can do that. She's a very experienced hunter and could possibly do it for you, but I don't think she could train you to do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Me of course!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, okay. So I just bring a broav to you and you can train it?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Pretty much, but first you'll need to catch one. You'll need to set up a pitfall trap, but instead of luring the broav over it, you'll just want to bait the trap with a fungus from Mort Myre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mort Myre? That's quite the trip.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Well luckily for you, I have one here I can spare you. You'll need to make some room for it first though.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Well luckily for you, I have one here I can spare you. Take it and make yourself a trap over at the old trapping spot to the west. You'll have a broav caught in no time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

sections.title: Using the dirty shirt on the broav before training it

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay boy, go get 'em!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's alright boy, take your time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Slow and steady wins the race...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, this isn't going to work. I think I need to take this broav to a hunting expert to get it woken up.

sections.title: Talking to the hunting expert with a broav

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Whoah there, stranger, careful where you're walking. I almost clobbered you there; thought you were a larupia or something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think you could train this broav for me?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Not a problem. I'll get it woken up and then we can get started...

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: There we go. All done!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey! Intruder alert!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Ivy Sophista
lines.text: Hello there, friend. I hope things are going well for you. If you're in need of any help with... anything, perhaps you might want to talk to Thaerisk upstairs...

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk without Movario's notes

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again, Thaerisk.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Welcome back, [player name]. What news do you have?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found a hideout near the Fight Arena that I think belongs to Movario. I've already found a few interesting things inside, but I'm going to keep searching and see what else I can uncover.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Excellent work. I look forward to hearing the results.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk with Movario's notes

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again, Thaerisk.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. I'll be departing for Falador shortly. You should head over to McGrubor's Wood as soon as you can. We need to make sure Idria and the Guardians of Armadyl are safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll go there right away.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again, Thaerisk.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Welcome back, [player name]. What news do you have?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found a hideout near the Fight Arena that seems to belong to Movario. I found these interesting research notes inside that I think might be of use.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Excellent work, [player name]. These notes are very useful, albeit rather concerning as well. I'll let you keep hold of them. You'll probably want to read them yourself, if you haven't already.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. So what's our next move?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: While you were gone, our negotiations with the Temple Knights continued. Given their nature, they weren't entirely convinced on joining our alliance initially, but it seems we managed to convince them.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: As Taverley no longer seems to be safe, we've decided it best that we establish a base of operations for this new alliance at the White Knights' Castle in Falador.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds sensible. So are we heading over there now?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: I will be departing shortly, but I'd like you to head for the Temple of Ikov instead. We've lost contact with Idria and the Guardians of Armadyl.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hopefully it's no cause for concern, but given what happened here, I fear Lucien may be targeting them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll get over there right away.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Head for the hidden entrance to the temple at McGrubor's Wood. Once you find Idria, send her to me at the White Knights' Castle and then join us there yourself. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Mercenary mage
lines.text: Kill the Armadyl-worshipping scum!

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: It's a good job you showed up when you did. Those mercenaries nearly had us done for!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sent by Lucien, no doubt. Thaerisk suspected something like this would happen.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: So I take it you're [player name]? I'm Idria. I've heard a lot about you. Much of it good... though it remains to be seen if that's enough to rectify your actions in stealing the Staff of Armadyl from us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's why I'm here. I intend to fix that mistake no matter what. I hope that means something.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: It very much does. It's a rare trait to be able to admit a mistake and work to fix it, though truth be told, it seems likely that Lucien would have obtained the Staff no matter what.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: But anyway, that's the past. We must focus on the future, and on ensuring said future is free of Lucien.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: So I take it you're [player name]? I've heard a lot about you. It's a pleasure to finally meet you. I'm Idria.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pleased to meet you, Idria.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: If Thaerisk sent you, I'm guessing you're more up to speed on the current state of things than I am. What can you tell me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Temple Knights have agreed to join the alliance. They're establishing a headquarters in the White Knights' Castle for us to use. Thaerisk asked us to meet him there.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Good. The more allies we have, the better. Now, given that Lucien is quite keen to see us dead, we shouldn't waste any more time here. I'll head to the White Knights' Castle as quick as possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll see you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Hello there. I'd love to stop and chat, but I'm afraid I have some important work to get done.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk, Idria, or Akrisae

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Ah, there you are, [player name]. Idria told us what happened at McGrubor's Wood. I'm glad to see you made it here safely. Now, allow me to introduce Akrisae Kolluym of the Temple Knights.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Glad to have you with us, [player name]. I've heard a lot about you from Sir Tiffy. It's Saradomin's will that you're doing!

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Perhaps it would be best if we kept theology out of this for now. While I'm sure all of us here would love to debate our differing viewpoints, we need to keep our focus on Lucien.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: A very fair point. My apologies.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Anyway, now that we're all here, I think it's time we discussed the contents of the notes that [player name] found. To put it bluntly, these notes confirm all of our worst fears.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: It seems that Lucien is indeed attempting to follow in Zamorak's footsteps and attain godhood. The Staff of Armadyl was merely the first step in his plan, for he now seeks an even greater prize.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: What could be more powerful than the Staff?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: There is an artefact that history has long forgotten. One with a power unlike anything else ever seen in Gielinor. A power that even the gods themselves struggle to comprehend.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: This power you speak of... You refer to the power found in the Temple of the Eye?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Yes. The Eye of Saradomin is one name for this artefact, though it has had many others. For example, we in Crux Eqal know it as the Fist of Guthix. However, its real name is...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Stone of Jas.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: You speak like you have first-hand experience, [player name]. Surely you have not encountered this artefact?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not exactly, but I was there when the Temple of the Eye was first uncovered. I had some sort of vision of the Stone. I've somehow known its name ever since.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: A vision? Curious...

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: And this Stone... You really think it can grant Lucien godhood?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Movario seems to think so, and he looks to have been right about everything else involving the Stone so far.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: So what do we do?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: We must find Lucien and stop him by any means necessary.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: I agree, but unfortunately our spies have had little luck in finding him so far.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: I suspect he's somewhere in the Wilderness. Our informants believe he has been raising an undead army there at a concerning speed. It will likely rival the zombie armies of Zemouregal before long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Wilderness is a big place. Too big. We'll need a more accurate location before we can do anything.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: I wonder if I might have a solution to that...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you thinking?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: According to Movario's notes, Lucien sent a pair of spies to keep an eye on him. If we can capture one of them, they might be able to lead us to him.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Ah, yes. We should have everything we need here to conduct an interrogation.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: It might require quite the interrogation to make one of Lucien's dogs talk.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: That depends on how loyal they are. Most of Lucien's followers seem to be mere mercenaries so far, but he does have some true believers as well.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: That said, we've recently managed to perfect a far more potent form of the traditional truth serum. I believe it should allow us to bypass any form of interrogating.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: That's all well and good, but how are we going to capture one of these spies?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: I reckon that is something I can help with. Silif, do you have the teleorb?

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: I have it right here, sir.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: [player name], given your experience in the field, I believe you'll be best placed for this task. Take this orb and plant it on one of Lucien's spies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is that thing? A commorb?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Not quite. It's a teleorb containing a sophisticated teleport spell that's directly linked to this cell here. Once planted on the spy, we can use it to bring them right to us.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Oh that is clever!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, where can I find these spies?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Provided Movario's notes are accurate, they should be somewhere in Draynor Village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll get to it.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: If you want, I can teleport you to Draynor Village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Very well. Let me know if you change your mind.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk after receiving the teleorb

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. If you need a hand with anything, you should talk to Akrisae.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me a bit more about yourself, Thaerisk.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Not much to tell, really. I grew up in Taverley and spent most of my life around druids. It seemed only natural for me to join them, and ten years ago that's exactly what I did.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: After that, I travelled around most of Gielinor and saw tragedy and heartache, triumph and beauty.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: I came back with a feeling that many of my fellow druids were a bit too absorbed in local matters and not spending enough time looking at the larger picture.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that's what led to Crux Eqal?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Yes. It turns out I wasn't quite alone in my feelings. I found a few likeminded druids and Crux Eqal was formed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about the Stone of Jas?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Far more than most I suspect, but truth be told, that's still very little.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: To us druids, it is known as the Fist of Guthix. It is a divine creation that allows one to unlock powers beyond what even the gods possess.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: It was misused by many during the God Wars, so when Guthix awoke, he hid it away so that none would ever be able to steal its power for themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you don't know where it was hidden?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: No. That is a secret that only Guthix himself now knows. I hope it stays that way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of Akrisae?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: He is very capable, but he reminds me of some of the druids back home. The ones that ultimately led us to form Crux Eqal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: He puts a lot of faith in his god. I don't necessarily fault that. As a loyal servant of Guthix, it would be very hypocritical of me to do so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: But Saradomin is long gone from this world, along with all the other gods. As their followers, we must be prepared to act without their guidance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of Idria?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: It is hard to form an opinion on her. She is very secretive. However, I sense a great sadness in her. Many of her order have already fallen victim to Lucien. I suspect she feels personally responsible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well if all goes well, they'll be avenged soon.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: I wonder if that in itself is something she feels conflicted over. Armadyl is a god of justice, not revenge. And yet, to lose those close to you...

sections.title: Talking to Idria after receiving the teleorb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Idria.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. How are things going with that spy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Well if you need anything, just talk to Akrisae.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. How are things going with that spy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've managed to plant the orb!

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Excellent work! You should let Akrisae know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you mind if I ask you a bit about yourself?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: To be honest, I'd prefer if you didn't. In our line of work, it is sometimes better not to give too much information away, especially when dealing with a threat like Lucien.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But do you not think it would be beneficial for us to know a bit more about each other?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: If you're worried about my ability to work with you and the others, I promise you have nothing to worry about. Now, let us discuss something else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about the Stone of Jas?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: I must admit, we had always wondered if the Staff of Armadyl was truly one of a kind, or if there might in fact be other artefacts out there of a similar power.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: However, until now, it was all just speculation. We'd never heard of this Stone before, so to be honest, I couldn't really tell you a thing about it.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: To think that it was out there for all this time... It worries me. It makes me question what else we don't know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of Akrisae?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: He's a Saradominist through and through.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that a problem?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Not necessarily. I'd say Saradominist and Armadylean beliefs are more aligned than those of most gods.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Still, I find Saradominists can be somewhat stubborn and unwilling to compromise. That could be a problem. If this alliance is to work, we'll need to set aside our egos.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of Thaerisk?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: I find that too many Guthixians are idealists. They prefer to imagine a perfect world rather than accept the one we currently live in.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: I know all too well the danger of that mindset. Our order was once the same, and we lost the Staff of Armadyl as a result.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Thaerisk on the other hand... He seems to understand that only through action can we ensure the safety of this world. We need more people like him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Good luck out there.

sections.title: Talking to Akrisae after receiving the teleorb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, Akrisae.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Saradomin be with you, my friend. Would you like me to teleport you to Draynor Village?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Very well. Let me know if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Saradomin be with you, my friend. You should head over to Draynor Village and see if you can get that orb planted.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me a bit more about yourself?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Well I shouldn't say too much. Protocol and all that sort of thing. The more you give away, the more your enemies can use against you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It would be useful to know a few details though, considering we're now working together.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Very true. Well, as you already know, I am a member of the Temple Knights, although I'm not actually a knight myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Normally to have reached my level of seniority, you'd have been knighted long ago. However, I was a priest originally, and technically still am.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: A priest cannot also hold a knighthood, and so I am the one Temple Knight in a leadership role who doesn't have one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's an interesting rule.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Isn't it just. Our order is known for having a few oddities like that, as I'm sure you yourself are aware. I hear your own recruitment involved a loophole or two.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's one way of putting it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about the Stone of Jas?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: The Eye is what we know it as. After the recent rediscovery of the Temple of the Eye, we put a great deal of effort into learning more about it. However, we didn't actually end up finding much.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: All we really know is that Saradomin used it to great effect in the God Wars. With it, he was able to protect this world from the evil of Zamorak.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But it seems Saradomin wasn't the only one to use it.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Yes, that is quite a concerning development, but this is the first time I've heard these other names that it has been given. I wonder if there might be a misunderstanding somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do you not believe what Movario discovered?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: I just think it's important to keep an open mind. With how little is known about the Eye... or Stone if you prefer, we need to be very careful of any misinformation, intentional or otherwise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of Idria?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: It is good to be working with her. Saradomin and Armadyl are natural allies. Just as the two of them once opposed Zamorak together, we will now oppose Lucien in the same way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's quite something having the followers of multiple different gods all working together like this.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: I suppose it's the one thing we can actually give Lucien credit for. He has united us in a way that few others could manage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of Thaerisk?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: It is refreshing to see a druid that is willing to stand up and take action. However, I do worry he may not have the stomach for this type of work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: As a Temple Knight yourself, I have no doubt you understand that we sometimes need to make difficult choices for the good of this world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Saradomin be with you, friend.

sections.title: Talking to Silif after receiving the teleorb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Ah, hello, [player name]. It's a pleasure to be working with you. Your exploits are, of course, legendary.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Don't mention it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: Oh, nothing much. I'd just heard Draynor was a nice place to visit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Suspicious outsider
lines.text: That's not really any of your business, is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

sections.title: Talking to the group after planting a teleorb

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Welcome back, [player name]. Any luck with that orb?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, the orb is planted!

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Saradomin seems to be smiling on our operation so far!

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: I'm sure that Guthix had just as much of a role in this as anyone else did.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: I thought we agreed to keep theology out of this? Besides, I'd say this work was exclusively [player name]'s doing.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Yes, of course, of course. Now, I believe it's time we invite one of Lucien's friends over for a little chat!

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: What the...?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Success!

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Over to you, Thaerisk.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Now, before we begin the interrogation... could I interest you in a refreshing beverage?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Beverage?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Yes, of course! We may have this man imprisoned, but we should still show some decorum!

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: *whispers* Play along...

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: I have one of my druids preparing you some fine juice as we speak.

lines.speaker: Druid
lines.text: I have the drink you requested, sire.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Excellent! Now, if you could give it to our friend here...

lines.speaker: Druid
lines.text: As you wish...

lines.speaker: Druid
lines.text: A drink, sir?

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: And this is... juice?

lines.speaker: Druid
lines.text: Uh... yes?

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: Did you really think I'd fall for that?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: The potion!

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Someone get a healer over here, quickly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are we going to do now?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Well, that spy ruined what truth serum we had...

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: So we don't have any more? This is a disaster! It should have been clear that old druid was going to be attacked! Why send him in there?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Now look...

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: We cannot afford to make these mistakes. The spy should have been restrained.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, you've all made your points, but we're not going to achieve anything like this. Thaerisk, can we make more of this potion?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: We're not just using any old truth serum here, so the process is quite complex. Still, it should be doable.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: First, we'll need some enriched snapdragon. Betty in Port Sarim should be able to help with that. You'll just need to take her a regular snapdragon seed.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Snapdragon seeds aren't the cheapest.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Well I do have one that I suppose I could part with. Let me know if you want it. I can also teleport you to Port Sarim if you wish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I have that seed?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Now, would you like me to teleport you to Port Sarim?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you teleport me to Port Sarim?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll make my own way there.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Very well.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk after the truth serum breaks

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. You'll need to take a snapdragon seed to Betty in Port Sarim. I have one here if needed. I can also teleport you to Port Sarim if you wish.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. You'll need to take a snapdragon seed to Betty in Port Sarim. I can teleport you there if you wish.

sections.title: Talking to Idria after the truth serum breaks

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Idria.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on getting us a replacement truth serum.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Well if you need anything, just talk to Thaerisk.

sections.title: Talking to Akrisae after the truth serum breaks

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: I hope we can get a replacement truth serum quickly. This delay is not what we need right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, Thaerisk and I are working on it.

sections.title: Talking to the shady stranger after the truth serum breaks

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: If you think I'm going to talk, you're kidding yourself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll see about that.

sections.title: Trying to open the cell gate

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Hello again. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that enriched snapdragon...

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: I've placed your snapdragon seed on the counter. You now need to enrich the seed using light from the doorway filtered through a rose-tinted lens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll get to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't have a rose-tinted lens.

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Well if you have your own lantern lens, then I can sell you the pink dye needed to colour it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much is it?

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: It's 20 coins per bottle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I don't have enough money with me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'll pass.

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Hello again. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you help me make some enriched snapdragon?

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Enriched snapdragon, eh? There's only one person who'd be wanting one of those. So you're working with Thaerisk now?

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Actually, on second thoughts, probably best I don't ask.

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: I can help you make the enriched snapdragon seed, but you'll then need to grow it yourself. If that's okay with you, you'll need to bring me a regular snapdragon seed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have one right here.

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Right, you now need to enrich the seed using light from the doorway filtered through a rose-tinted lens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll get to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't have a rose-tinted lens.

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Well I can sell you the colouring needed, but you'll have to provide your own lantern lens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In that case, I'll be back soon.

sections.title: Talking to Betty after creating the enriched seed

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Hello again. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that enriched snapdragon...

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: The seed is all ready for you. Just pick it up from the counter. After that, you'll need a specially prepared patch to grow it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. I'll have to ask Thaerisk about that. Thanks for the help, Betty.

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Don't mention it.

sections.title: Talking to Betty after taking the enriched seed

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Hello again. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that enriched snapdragon...

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I do with it now?

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Now you need to grow it, but you'll need a specially prepared patch to do so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. I'll have to ask Thaerisk about that. Thanks for the help, Betty.

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Don't mention it.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk with the enriched seed

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. Any luck with that snapdragon seed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it right here. What now?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: We've prepared a patch to grow it in. It's on top of the western tower of the castle. Head up there and get the seed planted. After that, I think Idria has a task for you.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk after planting the enriched seed

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. Any luck with that snapdragon seed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's growing as we speak.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Excellent! While we wait for it to grow, I think Idria has a task for you.

sections.title: Talking to Idria after planting the seed

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Ah, there you are, [player name]. I have a new task for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Provided all goes well with this replacement truth serum, we should soon know Lucien's location. However, given he now has the Staff of Armadyl, taking him on is not going to be easy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you thinking?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Thanks to our alliance, we can now bring a sizable force to bear. However, for Lucien himself, we require individuals with more specialised skills.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: With that in mind, we're looking to recruit a group of heroes to form a small task force. They will be the ones to deal with Lucien.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I take it you have some people in mind?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: We do. Your task is to seek out and recruit three legendary Slayer Masters: Turael, Mazchna and Duradel. Alongside them, we'd also like you to recruit a great warrior called Cyrisus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, Cyrisus! I'm glad to hear he's considered a great warrior now. Back when I first met him he needed a bit of help with that sort of thing.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Oh, so you're already acquainted? That should make things easier as we've actually found it quite hard to locate him. He always seems to be on the move.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: I have heard that the elusive Moon Clan have methods for contacting people across great distances. Perhaps that might allow you to easily reach out to Cyrisus and the others.

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about Lucien.

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: I will be joining you at the White Knights' Castle very soon. I just have a few things I need to do first.

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: 'Ello!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you doing, Turael?

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: I can honestly say that I'm very excited. It's been so long since I last did anything like this, and Lucien... Well, let's just say that this will be a fitting final adventure for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then what?

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: Well, being a Slayer Master was already a form of retirement, but I reckon it might finally be time for me to leave that behind as well.

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: No more needing to swap tasks for anyone! Can you imagine? The hours I've had to spend keeping records up to date! It will be nice not having to worry about that anymore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you think will take over from you?

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: Well my sister, Aya, is currently filling in for me while I'm here. Maybe she'll be open to doing that longer term. If not, my daughter, Spria, has already become a capable Slayer Master herself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what happens if you end up getting bored of this new retirement?

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: Well I was thinking I might take up viticulture. Someone needs to give Bernald some competition.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well if it takes off, I'll be sure to come and try some samples.

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're filling in for Turael?

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: That I am. It's been quite a while since I last did this sort of thing, but my brother wants one last adventure, so here I am.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you've done this before?

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: Oh yes. In fact, I was doing it before my brother was. Back in his adventuring days, I was the one here teaching people the basics.

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: After Spria was born, he retired from his life of thrill- seeking. That's when he took over from me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... I never knew that.

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: He doesn't talk about his adventures much, but once you get him going, you won't be able to make him stop!

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about Lucien.

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: Lucien, eh? Can't say that's a name I'm familiar with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's a Mahjarrat who's growing in power and is trying to become a god! We're forming an alliance against him at the White Knight's Castle. We'd like you to join us.

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: Truth be told, friend, this is basically my retirement. Still, I must admit, years of teaching amateurs how to kill goblins has had me itching to get back to the life of adventure...

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: Eh, you know what... I'm in! One last job, and quite the job it is!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's great, Turael. I'll see you in Falador.

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about Lucien.

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: Control your enthusiasm, friend. I will join you in Falador as soon as I am able.

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: 'Ello! Think you're ready to face Lucien then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess so, though I have a question for you: Why do all Slayer Masters say 'ello so much?

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: Ah, it's somewhat of a tradition in our order. Perhaps there was once some reason, but it's long been lost to time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't know the reason? That seems a bit odd to me.

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: Well who knows what would happen if we stopped? It would probably be fine, but can you really be sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess...

lines.speaker: Achtryn
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're filling in for Mazchna?

lines.speaker: Achtryn
lines.text: Indeed I am. It's been a while since he last asked me to step in, but it's good to get out and see the sights, to meet mortals and to generally socialise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do you know Mazchna well?

lines.speaker: Achtryn
lines.text: Oh, yes, he's such a mine of information. He hears what's going on at the pub, listens to the gossip of passing adventurers, and now he's off on a great adventure himself.

lines.speaker: Achtryn
lines.text: Though, of course, you know that already. Sorry to babble on. I'm sure you have plenty of other things to be doing.

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about Lucien.

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Mahjarrat.

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: Oh, him? I'm surprised that coward is still around to be honest. He was never worthy of being a Mahjarrat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd be careful of underestimating him. He's managed to claim the Staff of Armadyl and is quickly growing in power.

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: Hmm... This is concerning. What can I do to help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We've formed an alliance at the White Knights' Castle that we'd like you to join.

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: I would be glad to. The chance to face a Mahjarrat again is something I have desired for many centuries. I look forward to the carnage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Duradel
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about Lucien.

lines.speaker: Duradel
lines.text: I am ready to join you. I just need to make arrangements for while I am gone, but then I will depart for Falador.

lines.speaker: Duradel
lines.text: 'Ello! It's good to finally be out of that hut. After all these years, it's started to feel like a prison.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean you weren't permitted to leave? Who was stopping you? Who could stop you, for that matter?

lines.speaker: Duradel
lines.text: Let's just say that you don't become the greatest Slayer Master without treading on some very important toes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now, this is getting interesting. Do tell?

lines.speaker: Duradel
lines.text: I'm afraid it is a secret that will go to my grave, friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Kuradal
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're filling in for Duradel?

lines.speaker: Kuradal
lines.text: I am. The task he has been given is an important one, but this post must always be filled. Who better to fill it than his own daughter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Daughter? So being a Slayer Master runs in the family?

lines.speaker: Kuradal
lines.text: Of course. One cannot simply grow up with someone like my father. My childhood consisted of a lot of training. My father has always been among the best, but he wanted me to be even better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a lot of hard work.

lines.speaker: Kuradal
lines.text: Yes, but we wouldn't have it any other way.

lines.speaker: Duradel
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about Lucien.

lines.speaker: Duradel
lines.text: Is this regarding the Staff of Armadyl?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes... but how did you know about that?

lines.speaker: Duradel
lines.text: I would love to tell you, but I'm afraid I am sworn to secrecy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see... Well I'm hoping you'll be willing to join our alliance against him at the White Knights' Castle.

lines.speaker: Duradel
lines.text: Willing? Yes. However, I must first ensure that me leaving Shilo Village will not be a problem...

lines.speaker: Duradel
lines.text: It seems my years of waiting are over. I have been granted leave to join your cause. Let Lucien tremble, for he will hear my laughter as his corrupt soul burns in the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... Thanks, Duradel.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Well, this is all quite exciting. It's an illustrious group that you've invited me to join.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We're happy to have you along. You've become quite the warrior.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I suppose I've not done badly. Still, without you, I'd be nothing more than a coward. Thank you, friend. I won't let you down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Cyrisus, I need to talk to you about Lucien.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Ah, [player name]. Good to hear from you. Who's this Lucien you speak of?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's a Mahjarrat who's quickly growing in power. We need to stop him. Would you be willing to join a small party tasked with doing so?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: What other challenges are there for an adventurer? If there's help I can offer, then offer it I shall!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! We're gathering at the White Knights' Castle in Falador.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Then I shall join you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, that's all the heroes recruited. I should probably check in with Idria.

sections.title: Talking to Idria after recruiting the first set of heroes

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, Idria. I've recruited those heroes. They're all quite keen to take on Lucien.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: They are not the kind of people to let evil thrive. That is why we picked them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: You should check in with Thaerisk. Getting that truth serum sorted is our next priority.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk after speaking to Idria

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. I imagine that snapdragon seed will have grown by now. You should head on up and check on it.

sections.title: Talking to Akrisae after speaking to Idria

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Saradomin be with you, [player name]. How are things going with that truth serum?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I reckon the enriched snapdragon might have grown by now.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Hmm... Might be worth you going and checking on it.

sections.title: Talking to Idria after speaking to Thaerisk

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm nearly there with that replacement truth serum.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Well if you need any help with it, just talk to Thaerisk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Druid
lines.text: Good day to you. The enriched snapdragon is fully grown and ready for you to harvest.

sections.title: Talking to the druid after picking the herb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Druid
lines.text: Good day to you. You should take that enriched snapdragon to Thaerisk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, will do.

sections.title: Talking to the druid after losing the herb

lines.speaker: Druid
lines.text: Good day to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the enriched snapdragon I harvested.

lines.speaker: Druid
lines.text: Not to worry. There was some left over after you were done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk with the herb

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. I imagine that snapdragon seed will have grown by now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have the herb right here.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Excellent! I have a regular truth serum for you. Just add the herb to it and it should be ready to go. Given what happened before, it might be best if you administer it yourself this time.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk after receiving the truth serum

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. How are things going with that truth serum?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still need to make some.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Well you should just need to add the enriched snapdragon to a regular truth serum. Given what happened before, it might be best if you administer it yourself this time.

sections.title: Talking to Akrisae after receiving the truth serum

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Saradomin be with you, [player name]. How are things going with that truth serum?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We're nearly there now. I just need to prepare the serum.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Glad to hear it!

sections.title: Talking to Idria after receiving the truth serum

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Idria.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm nearly there with that replacement truth serum.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Well if you need any help with it, just talk to Thaerisk.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk with the serum

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. How are things going with that truth serum?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some right here.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Excellent! Given what happened before, it might be best if you administer it yourself this time. Just head into the cell when you're ready.

sections.title: Talking to Akrisae with the serum

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Saradomin be with you, [player name]. How are things going with that truth serum?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the replacement right here.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Praise Saradomin! May I suggest you be the one to give it to the spy this time so we avoid any more... accidents.

sections.title: Talking to Idria with the serum

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Idria.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some replacement truth serum.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Perfect! Let's get it administered and see what this spy knows.

sections.title: Searching the drawer for papyrus and charcoal
Talking to the shady stranger while inside the cell with the serum
Talking to the shady stranger again without the materials to make a sketch

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: Please... I can tell you what the Dark Squall looks like!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, let me grab some things to sketch him with and I'll be back.

sections.title: Talking to the shady stranger again with the materials to make a sketch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, tell me what this Dark Squall looks like.

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: He wears a hooded burgundy robe with purple trim and a strange diamond-shaped symbol on the front. Over the top he wears a tan jacket.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And his face?

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: He hides it with a red mask!

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: That's all I know, I promise!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Very well.

sections.title: Talking to the shady stranger again after sketching the suspect

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Remind me what this Dark Squall looks like.

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: He wears a hooded burgundy robe with purple trim and a strange diamond-shaped symbol on the front. Over the top he wears a tan jacket.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And his face?

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: He hides it with a red mask!

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: That's all I know, I promise!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Very well.

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: If you think I'm going to talk, you're kidding yourself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll see about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right then. We've tried this the easy way, so now we're doing it the hard way! Drink this serum or I'll run you through like the dog you are!

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: Oh, well, I guess I can cooperate... Down in one, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, it's time for you to talk. Where is Lucien?

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: I don't know! I've never even met him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then who gives you your orders?

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: They call him the Dark Squall. He's super secretive! I don't know his real name!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to need more than that!

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: Wait! I can tell you what he looks like!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, let me grab some things to sketch him with and I'll be back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go on...

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: He wears a hooded burgundy robe with purple trim and a strange diamond-shaped symbol on the front. Over the top he wears a tan jacket.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And his face?

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: He hides it with a red mask!

lines.speaker: Shady stranger
lines.text: That's all I know, I promise!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Very well.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk before making the sketch

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Any luck with the spy, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's willing to give me details on a person of interest.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Well if you need anything, I'm sure Akrisae can help.

sections.title: Talking to Idria before making the sketch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've given the truth serum to the spy, but I need to make a sketch of a person of interest.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Well if needed, I'm sure Akrisae will be able to help you with that.

sections.title: Talking to Akrisae before making the sketch

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Saradomin be with you, [player name]. How are things going with that spy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's prepared to give me details on a person of interest.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: You'll be wanting to make a sketch no doubt? Charcoal and papyrus are standard equipment in our line of work. There should be some nearby if you need them.

sections.title: Talking to the group after talking to the spy

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I've questioned the spy and he gave me a description of someone called the Dark Squall.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Dark Squall?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently the spy works for him rather than Lucien directly.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: I suppose it makes sense that Lucien would work through a proxy. Still, this doesn't give us a lot to go on.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: True, but we'll just have to work with what we've got. You said the spy gave you a description?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I made a sketch of what he described.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Let's have a look.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Yes, I reckon we can work with this. I'll arrange for some wanted posters to be put up.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: And I'll pass copies on to our spy network. Hopefully we'll have something before long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: We have three more heroes that we'd like you to recruit to our cause.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are we after this time?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Ghommal, Sloane and Harrallak. All three are members of the Warriors' Guild. Given their nature, we have no doubt they'll agree to join us once you explain the situation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, and I made a sketch... though I seem to have misplaced it.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: You'll need to make another then. We're going to need that sketch if we're to find this Dark Squall.

sections.title: Talking to Akrisae after getting the second recruitment task but before finishing recruitment

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Saradomin be with you, [player name]. I hear Idria has you recruiting more heroes to our cause. You should speak to her if you need help with anything.

sections.title: Talking to Idria after getting the second recruitment task

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. Have you managed to recruit those heroes yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Remember, you're aiming to convince Ghommal, Sloane and Harrallak to join us. All three are members of the Warriors' Guild.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk after getting the second recruitment task

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. I believe Idria has more heroes for you to recruit. You should speak to her about the details.

lines.speaker: Ghommal
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about Lucien.

lines.speaker: Ghommal
lines.text: I have already agreed to join your cause. I just need to make arrangements so we have someone to take care of the Guild while I'm gone.

lines.speaker: Ghommal
lines.text: When are we going to have this fight? I tire of waiting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We're not ready quite yet, but I'm sure it won't be long.

lines.speaker: Ghommal
lines.text: Good. This Mahjarrat will soon know pain!

lines.speaker: Ghommal
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about Lucien.

lines.speaker: Ghommal
lines.text: Lucien? Who's that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's a powerful Mahjarrat who's trying to achieve godhood!

lines.speaker: Ghommal
lines.text: Ah, yes, I remember this name now. Harrallak has spoken of him before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working with an alliance of people who are trying to stop him. We were hoping you would join us.

lines.speaker: Ghommal
lines.text: A chance to put down evil is a chance I will never refuse! You have my support.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! We've established a base of operations at the White Knights' Castle. Join us there when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Ghommal
lines.text: I will need to make arrangements with Laidee so we have someone to take care of the Guild. Once that is done, I will join you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, great, thanks!

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: Welcome to my humble Guild, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about Lucien.

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: Don't worry, friend. I will be joining you at the White Knights' Castle shortly.

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: Ah, it's good to get going on a real adventure again. The Guild takes up so much of my time these days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not apprehensive about the dangers ahead?

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: Absolutely, but I positively relish them as well. There's nothing like a bit of danger to get the blood pumping.

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: Welcome to my humble Guild, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about Lucien.

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: Lucien? You speak of the Mahjarrat?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know of him?

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: Oh yes. Many years ago, I was one of a group of heroes recruited by the Temple Knights to help stop his attack on Varrock.

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: Not that they really needed us. He was no Zemouregal, and his attack didn't really amount to much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm afraid he's grown a lot more powerful since then. He's managed to claim the Staff of Armadyl and is looking to ascend to godhood!

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: I see... That is disturbing news, my friend. I take it you seek my help in fighting against him once more?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. We've formed an alliance that's trying to stop him and would like you to join us.

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: My friend, it would be an honour. My sword is yours.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, Harrallak. When you're ready, head over to the White Knights' Castle.

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: I will join you there as soon as I'm able.

lines.speaker: Sloane
lines.text: Ah, hello there, warrior.

lines.speaker: Sloane
