lines.text: Ah, but I can see you are already as strong as is possible! Perhaps you have come to me to purchase a Cape of Strength, and thus join the elite few who have mastered this exacting skill?

lines.speaker: Sloane
lines.text: As an additional recognition of your achievement, the Cape of Strength can teleport you to our guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about Lucien.

lines.speaker: Sloane
lines.text: You have my axe, friend. I just need to finish up some things here, but then I will join you.

lines.speaker: Sloane
lines.text: Well, I'm ready and waiting to get going. Do you know how long it will be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure, but I don't expect it will be long.

lines.speaker: Sloane
lines.text: Then I shall use this time to train. After all, there's no such thing as being overprepared.

lines.speaker: Sloane
lines.text: Ah, hello there, warrior.

lines.speaker: Sloane
lines.text: Ah, but I can see you are already as strong as is possible! Perhaps you have come to me to purchase a Cape of Strength, and thus join the elite few who have mastered this exacting skill?

lines.speaker: Sloane
lines.text: As an additional recognition of your achievement, the Cape of Strength can teleport you to our guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about Lucien.

lines.speaker: Sloane
lines.text: Lucien? I've heard the name, but never in a good way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then you know the danger he poses. He's stolen the Staff of Armadyl and is trying to ascend to godhood!

lines.speaker: Sloane
lines.text: The Staff of Armadyl? I have heard tales of this artefact, but I assumed it to be a mere myth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not.

lines.speaker: Sloane
lines.text: Then it sounds like this evil must be brought to justice. What can I do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well we've formed an alliance at the White Knights' Castle that we'd like you to join. I warn you though, it could be dangerous.

lines.speaker: Sloane
lines.text: Like my father before me, and his father before him, I live by an ancient code, my friend. It is what led me to the Warriors' Guild and it drives me every day.

lines.speaker: Sloane
lines.text: I will not sit idly by and see evil flourish. Danger is nothing to a warrior who is true to his heart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So is that a yes?

lines.speaker: Sloane
lines.text: If by lending my axe to this quest, it offers a better chance of success, then I will see it through. I will stand with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glad to have you with us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, that's everyone recruited. I should probably head back to Falador.

sections.title: Talking to Idria after recruiting the second set of heroes

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. Good work recruiting those heroes. I reckon our task force is now complete.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! What happens now?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: You should speak to Akrisae. There has been a development that he can fill you in on.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk after recruiting the second set of heroes

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. Akrisae was hoping to speak with you.

sections.title: Talking to the Akrisae after all the heroes are assembled

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. Any luck finding Silif or the Dark Squall?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: We lost contact with Silif shortly after he entered the Black Knights' Fortress, so I'd suggest you start there.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still trying to find Silif and the Dark Squall.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Well if you need any specific details, I'm sure Akrisae can help.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. How goes the hunt for the Dark Squall?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Well if you need any help, just speak to Akrisae.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Ah, there you are, [player name]. There has been a potential development in our hunt for the Dark Squall, but I am concerned about...

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Hang on... did you hear that?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: It sounds like... footsteps? But really small ones...

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Er... come in?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hazelmere?!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Ah, I see you're already here, human. Good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand... What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Were you not paying attention when we last spoke? We find ourselves on the edge of a very dark future, one where the Mahjarrat deceiver is successful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lucien?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: He is a threat to all life. If he is not stopped, he will warp the Anima Mundi beyond recognition. As a guardian of the Anima Mundi, I must be here to ensure that doesn't happen.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: *whispers* Do you have any idea what they're doing?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: *whispers* I haven't the slightest clue...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure about this? Lucien is extremely dangerous!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: The Anima Mundi gave me a choice, and I have made it. If this is to be my last winter, then I go knowing the world will live on to see the next.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hazelmere, I...

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Er... Sorry to interrupt, [player name], but would you mind explaining who this is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah... yes, sorry. This is Hazelmere, one of the gnomes who created the Grand Tree in the Gnome Stronghold.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Ah, so you're Hazelmere! I've heard much about you!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah blah blah Guthix blah blah!

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Er... Sorry, I didn't quite catch that.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: *sigh* Ipmesi human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, so that's what that looks like from the outside.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Would you care to explain what's going on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hazelmere can mind meld with other people, allowing him to speak telepathically with them.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Does he not speak the common tongue?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not that he can't... He just chooses not to.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: How bizarre.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Right... I believe I need to make some introductions. If you'll excuse us...

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah blah!

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Well that was... interesting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... that's Hazelmere for you. Anyway, you said there had been a development.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: That's right. We had a reported sighting of someone matching the description of the Dark Squall near the Black Knights' Fortress.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: The Black Knights' Fortress? Could the Kinshra really have allied with Lucien?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Lord Daquarius is a devout Zamorakian. I doubt he would betray his god.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Not knowingly perhaps, but who's to say he knows Lucien's true ambition.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: A fair point. Regardless, I sent Silif to investigate, but we lost contact with him shortly after he entered the fortress.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go to the fortress. If the Dark Squall is there, I'll find him.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Good luck, [player name]. Don't forget to take some sort of disguise with you. I can't see you getting into the fortress otherwise. Oh, and keep an eye out for Silif as well. He may be in need of support.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: If you want, I can speed things up a bit by teleporting you to Ice Mountain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Very well. You know where I am if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Attempting to perform a mind meld on this many people would be a needless waste of energy, so I find myself forced to use your rudimentary language.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh come on, it's not that bad!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Hmph! How much longer are we going to be waiting anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have better things to be doing?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then don't be so impatient. Maybe try socialising or something?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Bah! No one here will have anything of interest to say. I'm better off conversing with myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you're sure...

Searching the northern wardrobe in the Dark Squall inner base

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is. I'm still working on getting you out of there.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: I wish I could help, but they roughed me up quite a bit... I'm struggling to think straight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll work something out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Silif, is that you?

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: What? I won't talk... You can't make me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Silif, it's me, [player name].

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: [player name]? Saradomin be praised!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to get you out of here, Silif. Any suggestions?

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Sorry, but I've no idea. They roughed me up quite a bit... I'm struggling to think straight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll work something out.

sections.title: Talking to Silif after unlocking his cell

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm back, Silif. I've managed to get the cell unlocked. We should be able to get you out of here now.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: I'm very weak... I can barely move...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Some food and a restoration potion should sort you out. I have both on me right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm back again, Silif. How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Not much better... I can still barely move...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I have some food and a restoration potion for you. They should sort you out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I have some food, but I'm still working on finding you a restoration potion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I have a restoration potion, but I'm still working on finding you some food.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]. However, this is quite embarrassing... but I don't think I have the strength to take them myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you need me to feed you?

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: I'm afraid so...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, hang tight.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Thank you, friend. I really needed that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, Silif, do you think you can move now?

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: I think so. Thanks, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, then let's get you out of here.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Not yet. My mission isn't complete.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A few moments ago you could barely even move. You're in no fit state to be worrying about your mission.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Then how about a compromise? My job was to find the Dark Squall and gather any intelligence available.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: If you can get me into the inner section of this place, I can memorise any intelligence and then get back to Falador, leaving the Dark Squall for you to handle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Very well.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]. I see you managed to find yourself a disguise to use. For this to work, I'll need one as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some armour here that should do the job.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Excellent! Now, excuse me for a moment...

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Right, shall we get to it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's go.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Very well. I'm right behind you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just give me a moment.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Very well, but don't take too long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In that case, I'll look around and see what I can find.

sections.title: Using the restoration potion on Silif directly

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Thank you, friend. I'm feeling a bit better already.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Thank you, friend. I'm feeling a bit better already.

sections.title: Dropping the elite black full helm
Talking to Silif after he has been restored and fed

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. Any luck finding that disguise?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: I'll wait here until you find something suitable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some armour here that should do the job.

sections.title: Leaving the Black Knight Catacombs while Silif is following the player
Talking to Silif after he returns to his cell

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Right, shall we get to it?

sections.title: Talking to Silif while following the player

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: We need to get into the inner section of this place. I'll follow your lead.

sections.title: Talking to Silif at the inner base

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: This is where we want to be! Let's see if we can find some sort of intelligence. Maybe some maps or similar.

sections.title: Leaving Silif by the map board

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Ah, this looks perfect. I'll stay here and study these maps while you deal with the Dark Squall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm guessing you had something in mind for him?

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Indeed. Remember how you apprehended that spy earlier? I think the same sort of thing should work perfectly here.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: I managed to keep a teleorb hidden when I was captured. If you can plant it on the Dark Squall, we can teleport him to Falador!

sections.title: Talking to Silif by the map board

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Have you managed to plant that teleorb on the Dark Squall yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: He must be somewhere nearby. Get it planted on him and then get yourself back to Falador.

sections.title: Talking to the Elite Black Knight by the ladder

lines.speaker: Elite Black Knight
lines.text: Intruder! Attack!

lines.speaker: Elite Black Knight
lines.text: That area is out of bounds, soldier! You know you can't go up there!

lines.speaker: Elite Black Knight
lines.text: Good day, master... Wait, I didn't see you come back down!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course you didn't, you meathead! I simply teleported away!

lines.speaker: Elite Black Knight
lines.text: Of course, master! My apologies!

lines.speaker: Elite Black Knight
lines.text: Good day, master... Wait, I didn't see you come back down!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course you didn't, you meathead! I simply teleported away! Now drop and give me five!

lines.speaker: Elite Black Knight
lines.text: Yes master!

sections.title: Climbing up the ladder in the inner base

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Powered staves are no match for my magic!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: I am unstoppable!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: My mind is pure arcanery!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: My magic transcends everything you know!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You face the greatest wizard ever to live!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Witness true sorcery!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Magicus ignitus!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Barrerus arcanicus!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Fighting you is beneath me!

lines.speaker: Strong Warrior
lines.text: My strength is yours, master!

lines.speaker: Strong Warrior
lines.text: Now!

lines.speaker: Strong Warrior
lines.text: Ha! None can break free from my strength!

lines.speaker: Agile Warrior
lines.text: My nimbleness is yours, master!

lines.speaker: Agile Warrior
lines.text: Now!

lines.speaker: Agile Warrior
lines.text: Too slow!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Magicus tremurus!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Ugh!

lines.speaker: Dark Squall
lines.text: Who in the world are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah... Well... I was sent here by Lucien to be your new... apprentice?

lines.speaker: Dark Squall
lines.text: That voice... I know who you are! You're [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... No I'm not...

lines.speaker: Dark Squall
lines.text: Ha! You have no idea who you're meddling with, do you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surok?!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You've been quite the problem for some time. I'll be glad to finally have rid of you...

sections.title: Planting the teleorb on Surok after defeating him

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you doing there, Surok?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Eugh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, you can look after this for me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably get back to Falador before he recovers.

sections.title: Attempting to climb the ladder to Surok after defeating him

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's probably best I don't go back up there again.

sections.title: Talking to Silif after rescuing him

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Silif.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Hello there, friend. Thanks for your help back at the Black Knight Catacombs. I'd probably still be locked in that cell if it wasn't for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem, Silif. I'm sure you'd do the same for me.

sections.title: After to the group after planting the teleorb on Surok

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Welcome back, [player name]. We've already heard about your heroics in those catacombs from Silif. Good work! Thanks to your help, he was able to gather some very useful information for us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's not all. I was also able to discover the true identity of the Dark Squall: Surok Magis.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: So it's not just the Kinshra. Lucien has managed to ally with the Dagon'hai as well.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: It's starting to seem like Lucien's allied with more Zamorakian organisations than he hasn't!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well when it comes to Surok, we might be able to deal with that problem fairly easily. I managed to plant a teleorb on him.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Excellent! That could be quite helpful when it comes to enacting our plan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Plan?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: This is the big one. We believe we are now in a place where we can move against Lucien.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: The task force is already in position, and thanks to the information Silif was able to obtain, we believe we now know where to find Lucien.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where?

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Some of the plans I found detailed a remote location on the western edge of the Wilderness. I reckon that's Lucien's base of operations.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do we find out for sure?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Well that's where you come in. We need you to scout out this location and confirm Lucien's presence. Once you have done so, the task force will move in and deal with him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how do I get there?

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: It seems the enemy has their own form of teleorb, similar to the ones we use ourselves. I found details suggesting that the Dark Squall, or Surok I should say, was in possession of one.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Surok is likely one of the few people who can directly reach Lucien. That orb is presumably how he does it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have an orb here that I found while exploring those catacombs. I'm guessing this is the one he uses.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Perfect! That's one less thing to worry about. There will be a location within Surok's headquarters where the orb must be used from. Find it and use the orb to reach Lucien.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find this orb?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: It's likely somewhere in Surok's headquarters. The same place you and Silif found that intelligence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And once I have the orb? Then what?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: There will be a location within Surok's headquarters where the orb must be used from. Find it and use the orb to reach Lucien.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: I'd recommend you keep that disguise on. It should let you get nice and close to Lucien without being spotted.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: I'd recommend disguising yourself again before you do so. That should let you get nice and close to Lucien without being spotted.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have Surok's Dark Squall robes with me right now. I can use them.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Right, I think that covers everything. When you're ready to go, just step inside the cell. We're going to use that teleorb you planted to swap your location with Surok's.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, good thinking.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Good luck, [player name].

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Let's finish this.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. When you're ready to go, just step inside the cell. We're going to use that teleorb you planted to swap your location with Surok's.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Are you ready, [player name]? I'll teleport you as soon as you enter the cell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Then let's do this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I need more time to prepare.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Very well. Just enter the cell when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. How goes the hunt for Lucien?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Don't worry, the heroes will be keeping an eye on things from a distance. They'll move in as soon as they have a positive sighting.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on finding Lucien.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Well good luck. I have no doubt you'll be successful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lucien!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's he doing now?

lines.speaker: Lucien
lines.text: You... Did you really think that disguise would fool me? You are not Surok... and you are not welcome!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: The human will be toast if we don't move now. Ready for teleport!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sloane! Duradel! No!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: This decision was already made and was for the best. Goodbye, my friends.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Get out of there! He's too powerful!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hazelmere!

lines.speaker: Lucien
lines.text: What wonderful devastation! Now, I have things to attend to.

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: We must find out where Lucien went! Turael and Duradel must be avenged!

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: Getting back to Falador would be wiser. Did you see the way he slaughtered us? That our weapons are useless? How many more would die? Some must survive to report this nightmare!

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: Such knowledge lost with their deaths... You are right. We have suffered enough for one day. We must retreat.

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: [player name], get back to Falador! We're teleporting out now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hazelmere... Cyrisus... What have I done? Lucien will pay for this!

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: [player name]! Thank goodness you're back. When Mazchna reported in, we feared the worst!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're gone... All of them... He killed them, like they were nothing!

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: We thought we knew how powerful Lucien had become. We thought we'd prepared appropriately...

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: I fear no amount of preparation would have been enough. We were never going to have a hope against that level of power...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He can't be allowed to get away with this!

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: But what can we do? It seems we already lack the ability to stand against Lucien, and he doesn't even have the Stone yet! With it...

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: He could bring an end to this world without even trying!

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: My friends, we cannot afford to lose hope now. The situation is dire, but one of our contacts recently reported in with information that may help us turn things around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Information? What information?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Movario has been sighted entering the caves below Lumbridge Swamp.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Lumbridge Swamp? There's nothing of value there, is there?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Nothing that we know of, but Movario has shown interest in the area before. He wouldn't be going back there now without good reason.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Surely it can't be...?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't think the Stone is down there, do you?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: If there's any chance that it is, then we must make sure he doesn't find it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I may have an idea. I could use my Dark Squall disguise to approach Movario.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can find out what he knows, and if the Stone is down there, I can hopefully reach it before he does.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: It's a risky plan, but I suspect it's the only one we've got.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Akrisae, do we have any information on where exactly in these caves Movario is?

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: He was last seen entering a large cavern filled with some mysterious floating lights.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can work with that. I'll head over there right away.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Good luck, [player name].

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Saradomin be with you, [player name]. How goes your mission?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't found Movario yet.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: According to our contact, he was seen entering a large cavern filled with mysterious floating lights somewhere within the Lumbridge Swamp Caves.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Don't forget to disguise yourself as the Dark Squall before you approach him. We need to convince him you're someone he can trust.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the heroes...

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: They will always be remembered, my friend. I've already made arrangements to have statues built in their honour. Everyone will know of their bravery, and their sacrifice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Statues? I think they'd have liked that. Thank you, Akrisae.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Good to see you, [player name]. How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't found Movario yet.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Akrisae said he'd been seen in the Lumbridge Swamp Caves. There was mention of a large cavern filled with mysterious floating lights.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Just remember, you'll need to disguise yourself as the Dark Squall. That should convince Movario that you're working with him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the heroes...

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Lucien will not be allowed to get away with his actions, my friend. We will stop him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you believe in revenge?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Revenge? No. However, I do believe in justice. I know some druids would even challenge that, but a world without justice is a world without balance.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Lucien, and those like him, must be held accountable for their actions. It falls to people like us to ensure that happens. It's the only way the world will stay safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just hope we can stop him soon.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: We will, my friend. With people like you fighting for our cause, Lucien has no hope.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. Any luck with Movario?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing yet.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: According to Akrisae, he was last seen somewhere in the Lumbridge Swamp Caves, in a large cavern filled with mysterious floating lights.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: If you disguise yourself as the Dark Squall, you'll hopefully be able to convince him you're working with him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the heroes...

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: They will never be forgotten, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It just feels like they died for nothing.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: I know how you feel, but the sacrifices we make... it's never for nothing. They were willing to stand against a great evil so that the world might be safe.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: That's how I know that Lucien will never win. We fight for something that he will never understand, and we do so no matter the chance of success.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Because of that, our sacrifices will always have far more meaning than anything he stands for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, Idria. That means a lot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Movario?

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: What's it to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing, I guess.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Then be gone, before I set Darve on you!

sections.title: Talking to him again in disguise

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Ah, so you've returned. Ready to take your trip to the bottom yet?

lines.speaker: Darve
lines.text: Can we hurry things up? I've got better things to do than hang around these filthy caves!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shut up, Darve!

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Well said!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Remind me what I need to do.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Just attract one of the light creatures using one of those lanterns you were talking about. When you're in position, I'll use my light spell to send you down to the bottom of the chasm.

lines.speaker: Darve
lines.text: I hope that Surok character doesn't come back. There's something not right about him... He makes General Khazard seem normal!

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: If you don't like him, be glad you've never met Lucien.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Movario?

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: What's it to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing, I guess.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Then be gone, before I set Darve on you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Movario?

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: What's it to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lucien has sent me to check on your progress.

lines.speaker: Darve
lines.text: Movario! That's...

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Yes, Darve, I can see who it is. Let it be known, Surok, that I am not one of your minions. I will not bow down to your attempts to intimidate me.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: As for my progress, you can tell Lucien that the investigations would be greatly expedited if I weren't subject to all these interruptions!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then allow me to expedite things myself. Time is now of the essence and Lucien's patience is wearing thin.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: You would take over? Preposterous!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: While Lucien has asked me to oversee your investigations, I am not unreasonable. Believe it or not, I've actually read much of your detailed research myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You are clearly an expert in this area, and I am open to letting you continue to take the lead, provided we are able to quickly conclude our work here.

lines.speaker: Darve
lines.text: Looks like we don't have much choice, boss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now, please share what you know with me.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Hmph. Very well. Given you have read my research, and given Lucien himself sent you, I trust you know what it is I seek.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Even with all that I know, I can still scarcely even imagine it! A power beyond even the gods themselves!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am all too aware, but all this means little given you have still not found it.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Oh, but I believe I have found it. More than that, I believe we are stood right above it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right above it? Surely you don't mean the chasm?

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: That's precisely what I mean. Perhaps now you see the issue that I face. Reaching the bottom will require a significant amount of effort.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: I attempted to recruit some goblins to find a way down, but they proved... inadequate. I fear a gantry will need building, which will not be a quick process.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what about the light creatures? Are they of no use?

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: I don't think so... though I suppose they are rather unique. Perhaps I shall study their movements at some point.

lines.speaker: Darve
lines.text: I think Surok has an idea for using them, boss!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Allow me to explain. This isn't my first time in these caves. On a previous visit, I found that a special type of lantern with a distinct blue light could attract the creatures.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Once attracted, they'd surround you and carry you to the far side.

lines.speaker: Darve
lines.text: You wouldn't get me in one of those things! Not on your life!

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Shut up, Darve! The adults are talking!

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Did you ever manage to get them to go downwards? To the bottom?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. I assume the lantern isn't bright enough for that.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Hmm, intriguing! I know of a light spell which might work. If you were to use your lantern to call a creature, I could try and use my spell to send it down to the bottom of the chasm!

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: However, that does then pose a problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Well it would mean you'd be going down first! After all this work, do you really think I'm just going to let you get your hands all over my finds?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your finds? Is that how Lucien would describe them? What do you think he will do if I inform him of your lack of cooperation?

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Okay, fine! Given I have no choice, when you're ready to go down, just attract a light creature. Once you're in position, I'll use my light spell.

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: Watch out, surface-dweller! Don't get too close to the edge!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: Movario's paying us to help him with his expedition. He thin-

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Goblin! What did I tell you about what we're doing here?

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: That it's a secret... which means we shouldn't tell strangers about it.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: That's right!

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: Watch out, surface-dweller! Don't get too close to the edge!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: Movario's paying us to help him with his expedition. He thinks there's something important down this chasm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how's that going so far?

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: Well Ulgon's climbing down to see if he can find the bottom. He didn't reach it last time, so Movario made us get a longer rope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Well good luck.

lines.speaker: Darve
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to speak to Movario.

lines.speaker: Darve
lines.text: Why you talking to me then? He's right there.

lines.speaker: Darve
lines.text: Don't think I can help the likes of you! Talk to Movario.

lines.speaker: Darve
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Darve
lines.text: Then get lost!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: You play with fire, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean Movario? Don't worry, I'm trying to stop him.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Are you sure your intentions are pure? Look into your heart. The power Movario and his master seek, a part of you wants this power for yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I promise, my only intention is to stop Movario and Lucien. I won't let them find what they're looking for.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I hope you are right, [player name].

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Tell me... a story...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About Movario...

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: You must stop him and his master. This is a quest that matters above all others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I won't let them find what they're looking for. I promise.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I hope you are right, [player name].

sections.title: Arriving at the bottom of the chasm

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... Some of the the light creatures followed me! I doubt I'll need that light spell to navigate down here again.

sections.title: Searching the skeletal remains (hammer and chisel)
Using the air orb on the air recess
Using the water orb on the water recess
Using the earth orb on the earth recess
Using the fire orb on the fire recess
Using an orb on the wrong recess
Inserting the air block into the air mechanism
Inserting the water block into the water mechanism
Inserting the earth block into the earth mechanism
Inserting the fire block into the fire mechanism
Inserting an elemental block into the wrong mechanism
Talking to Movario after arriving at the bottom of the chasm

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: You've returned! What news of the bottom? What's down there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, it's very dark down there. I can hardly see anything! It's going to take more work for me to get anywhere.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Well then let me help! Tell me, how do we summon the light creatures? I made one of those blue lanterns, but they still don't seem to come!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure. It worked fine for me.

lines.speaker: Darve
lines.text: It's that big snake over there! I keep telling you, every time you start enticing one, it does something to them! Why don't you listen to me?

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: It seems highly unlikely that a snake has that capability. That thing is nothing more than a bloated earth worm! Now silence, Darve!

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: As for you, what can you tell me about the chasm?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing much. Like I say, it's very dark. Now, I'm sorry, but I've answered your questions. You should let me get back to work.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: The impertinence! I'll ask these same questions until I get the answers I need! Now tell me, what's down there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, it's very dark down there. I can hardly see anything! It's going to take more work for me to get anywhere.

sections.title: Talking to Idria after entering the chasm

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. Any luck with Movario?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He thinks the Stone is hidden in a chasm deep below the rest of the caves. I've managed to get down there myself, but I haven't found anything of note yet.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Well then you'd better get back there. If Movario is close to finding the Stone, then we need to find it first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He thinks the Stone is hidden in a chasm deep below the rest of the caves. I've managed to get down there myself and I've found some sort of Guthixian temple.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Well then you'd better get back there. If Movario is close to finding the Stone, then we need to find it first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found the Stone! It's in a chasm deep below the rest of the caves.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Well then you need to get back there now and make sure the Stone is safe! I have a party that's nearly ready to support you. We will join you as soon as we can.

sections.title: Talking to Akrisae after entering the chasm

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Saradomin be with you, [player name]. How goes your mission?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Movario thinks the Stone is hidden in a chasm deep below the rest of the caves. I've managed to get down there myself, but I haven't found anything of note yet.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Well keep it up, [player name]. I have no doubt you'll lead us to success.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Movario thinks the Stone is hidden in a chasm deep below the rest of the caves. I've managed to get down there myself and I've found some sort of Guthixian temple.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: A Guthixian temple? Curious... You'd better get back there as soon as you can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found the Stone! It's in a chasm deep below the rest of the caves.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: You must speak to Idria at once! She has been preparing a party to support you.

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk after entering the chasm

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Good to see you, [player name]. How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Movario thinks the Stone is hidden in a chasm deep below the rest of the caves. I've managed to get down there myself, but I haven't found anything of note yet.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: You'd better get back there as soon as possible, [player name]. We can't let Movario find the Stone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Movario thinks the Stone is hidden in a chasm deep below the rest of the caves. I've managed to get down there myself and I've found some sort of Guthixian temple.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: A Guthixian temple? This is concerning, [player name]. You'd better get back there as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found the Stone! It's in a chasm deep below the rest of the caves.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Well then you must get back there as soon as you can! We can't let Lucien get the Stone.

lines.speaker: Balance Elemental
lines.text: In the name of Guthix, what rights do you have here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know who you are... or what you are for that matter, but we don't have much time! The Stone is in danger!

lines.speaker: Balance Elemental
lines.text: The Stone is always in danger. That is why I stand before you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know my name?

lines.speaker: Balance Elemental
lines.text: I know everything about you, [player name]. I have seen your past, your present and your future.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then you must have seen Lucien and his power! We have to get the Stone to safety!

lines.speaker: Balance Elemental
lines.text: Your duty is noble, [player name], but so is mine: To protect the Stone until a time when, near to death, you are relieved of all duties with the gratitude and blessings of Guthix.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you won't help me?

lines.speaker: Balance Elemental
lines.text: I am not here to help. I wish you all the best, [player name], but I am charged with a singular purpose, and I will now do my duty...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: I still don't understand, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was V who first found it, deep below the ice of Lunar Isle. A single touch granted him the power of the gods. The power of limitless creation...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Over the centuries, it changed hands many times. For each person who'd use it for good, another would use it to destroy...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: And the kin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They were bound to it long ago. To use the Stone causes them unimaginable pain, and grants them immense power.

lines.speaker: Robert the Strong
lines.text: So the only way to win...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is the Stone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What in the...?

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: The Stone! After all this time... I've finally found it!

lines.speaker: Darve
lines.text: And look who's here as well! The so-called Lord Surok!

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Yes, quite a clever ploy, [player name], but you should have known I'd work it out. Now, step aside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And let you take the Stone? I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: We stand before the most important, the most significant, the most powerful artefact to ever be discovered in Gielinor, and it's all thanks to me!

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: With the patience of a thousand men, I have spent my life following the smallest of clues and the slightest of hints to discover only the most minute inkling of its existence!

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: So yes, you will step aside, because after all I've done to get here, I have no intention of letting you stop me now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If Lucien gets the Stone, the world burns. I don't plan on letting that happen.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: And what makes you think Lucien will ever lay his hands on it? He may have contributed to my research funds, certainly, but in terms of actual possession, I now hold the cards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'd be a fool to cross him. He'd wipe you from existence before you could even say a word.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Before, yes, but now... Now the power of the Stone is in the palm of my hand. Lucien has no hope of standing against that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Except you don't have the Stone yet. You'll have to get through me first.

lines.speaker: Darve
lines.text: No problems! I'll easily deal with you, mate!

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Darve, I wouldn't be too enthusiastic about tangling with our friend here. His strength is... unusual. Surely you didn't...

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Hang on... I detect a build up of energy...

lines.speaker: Lucien
lines.text: Movario.

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Lord Lucien! We did not expect...

lines.speaker: Lucien
lines.text: It seems you have found the Stone. Why was I not informed?

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: The discovery was made mere moments ago, my lord. Now I need time to conduct research and ensure it's safe to...

lines.speaker: Lucien
lines.text: Silence! Do you not feel that, Movario? It calls to me... my birthright!

lines.speaker: Lucien
lines.text: Icthlarin... Zaros... Zamorak... All of them false! Finally the Mahjarrat shall take their rightful place as the sole inheritors of Gielinor, and I will be their god!

lines.speaker: Lucien
lines.text: Any who stand in my way shall be swept away like the civilisations of old. For those that are lucky, their children shall become slaves!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wouldn't be so sure about that!

lines.speaker: Lucien
lines.text: Ah, [player name], I see you have decided to interfere once again. Commendable, but meaningless. You cannot stop what is to come. Death is all that awaits you if you try.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'll never have the satisfaction of killing me, Lucien. No matter what it takes, I'll make it my mission to stop you.

lines.speaker: Lucien
lines.text: Such arrogance. Do you not realise who you address? I am Lord Lucien of the Mahjarrat, Master of the Thousand Curses. Look upon my works and despair!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can bestow as many titles upon yourself as you like, but before long, no one will even remember your name. You're just another monster, craving a power you'll never have.

lines.speaker: Lucien
lines.text: Are you truly so misguided that you cannot see the truth before you? I hold both the Staff of Armadyl and the Stone of Jas!

lines.speaker: Lucien
lines.text: Not since Zamorak himself has anyone possessed this much power! Nothing will stop me following in his footsteps!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You think Zamorak will just stand by and let that happen?

lines.speaker: Lucien
lines.text: He doesn't have a choice. He no longer has any power here. His days are already done.

lines.speaker: Lucien
lines.text: Now, this conversation bores me. My ascension approaches, and I only have room for the strongest as my worshippers. Those too weak to slay even these lowly beasts will certainly not be wanted!

lines.speaker: Movario
lines.text: Time for us to leave, Darve!

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: We're with you, [player name]!

sections.title: Talking to Akrisae before killing the tormented demons

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: [player name], you must return to the temple below Lumbridge! Idria and her Guardians of Armadyl have already departed to support you!

sections.title: Talking to Thaerisk before killing the tormented demons

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: [player name], you must return to the temple below Lumbridge! Idria and her Guardians of Armadyl have already departed to support you!

sections.title: Talking to Idria after killing the tormented demons

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: That was astounding, [player name]! Good work!

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Blessed Armadyl, that was incredible! The Stone must have augmented your strengths. I've never seen anything like it!

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Now, we'd better get back to Falador. Do you want me to teleport you there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks. I'll make my own way back.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Very well. I'll see you back there.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: You're back! Is everything okay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not exactly. I'm sorry... I failed. Lucien has the Stone.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Then our worst fears have come to pass. The horrors that he could unleash with it...

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: That's something he'll never have a chance to do. Yes, this is a dark day, but I don't intend to give up, and I'm sure none of you do either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what do we do?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: [player name], you've helped forge an alliance between the Temple Knights, Crux Eqal and the Guardians of Armadyl. Three very different groups, all fighting for the common good.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: We must build on that. We will strengthen our forces, and find new allies to join our cause. When Lucien dares to rear his head again, we'll be ready.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Well said. When times are darkest, it is all the more important that we continue to look for the light. The Temple Knights stand ready.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: As do we in Crux Eqal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about me?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Rest, my friend. You have well and truly earned it. We will take things from here.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Don't worry, [player name]. When the time is right, you'll be the first to know. I promise you, we will find Lucien. Together, we will stop him.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: And the world will be safe once more.

lines.speaker: Sakirth
lines.text: Zorgoth?

lines.speaker: Sithaph
lines.text: Dead.

lines.speaker: Strisath
lines.text: Then we are free at last!

lines.speaker: Sakirth
lines.text: And a strength grows within us!

lines.speaker: Sithaph
lines.text: The Stone has been uncovered. The mortals try to claim its power.

lines.speaker: Sakirth
lines.text: Then let us bring terror and pain to them!

lines.speaker: Strisath
lines.text: Yes! The dragonkin will rise once more!

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Hello there, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, Idria. How are things here?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: We're still trying to work out what Lucien's next move is, but so far everything has been quiet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think he's doing?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: It's hard to say. He has the Stone of Jas and the Staff of Armadyl now. That should give him everything he needs to follow in Zamorak's footsteps, but for whatever reason, he's chosen not to act yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I can do?

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: Not really at this point, but we'll be sure to let you know the moment he dares try anything. In the meantime, you might want to return to that temple beneath Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: It seems the magics Lucien used down there were far more than just a single summoning spell. The area has been permanently desecrated by those horrifying demons.

lines.speaker: Idria
lines.text: If you were looking for some way of keeping Lucien and his forces in check, killing a few of those demons wouldn't be a bad idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might just do that. Thanks, Idria.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, Akrisae.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: Saradomin be with you, [player name]. We have a lot to be grateful to you for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, but I'm not sure I feel the same way. I wasn't able to stop Lucien.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: But you came very close and you lived to tell the tale. Lucien may have gained the upper hand, but he's not safe by any means.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: He has people like you opposing him, not to mention an alliance between the Temple Knights, Crux Eqal and the Guardians of Armadyl!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You make a good point. Let's just hope it will be enough.

lines.speaker: Akrisae
lines.text: It will be, my friend.

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Ah, [player name], it's good to see you again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, Thaerisk. Any word on Lucien?

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: Nothing yet, but we're doing everything we can to find him. I hope he's ready for us, because once we do locate him, we're going to give him quite the bloody nose...

lines.speaker: Thaerisk
lines.text: ... or at least whatever passes for a nose on a Mahjarrat!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Silif.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Hello there, friend. I heard what happened with Lucien and the Stone. Don't worry, he'll pay for his crimes sooner or later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hope you're right, Silif.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Have faith, friend. Evil never prospers for long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost those robes I was using as a disguise.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Not to worry. I have them here for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Silif
lines.text: Until next time, friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you enjoying your cell, Surok?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You let me out of here! This isn't over...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's nice to see a little justice around here!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Good day, Proselyte. I heard from Akrisae what happened with that Lucien business. Be sure to keep your chin up. We'll get the old boy sooner or later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, Tiffy.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Don't mention it, old bean. Now, is there anything I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About Turael...

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: You need not share your condolences with me, adventurer. I've been prepared for this for a long time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have?

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: My brother was a thrill-seeker his whole life. Even after he retired, I always knew something would come and pull him back in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that something was me. I'm sorry...

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: Don't be. He died doing what he loved, and he died a hero. He wouldn't have had it any other way.

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About Turael...

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: It's nothing personal, but I'd prefer not to discuss what happened with my father.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. Sorry for bringing it up.

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: Welcome to my humble Guild, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you, Harrallak?

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: I've lost two of my dear friends, [player name]. This old place doesn't seem to be the same since they're gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I feel responsible.

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: Nonsense, my friend! They both died bravely fighting against a great evil! There is no blame on you for that.

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: Remember them for who they were and what they stood for. Mourn their loss, but never bear the burden of their death, for that is not what they would want.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what about vengeance?

lines.speaker: Harrallak Menarous
lines.text: Ah, now that is a topic we could debate for many moons, my friend. Still, regardless of the reason, know that when the time comes, I will fight beside you against Lucien once more.

lines.speaker: Yadech Strongarm
lines.text: Ah, hello there, warrior.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you wielding a tree branch?

lines.speaker: Yadech Strongarm
lines.text: Well... there's a story to that. You see, I should have an axe but-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sloane's axe?

lines.speaker: Yadech Strongarm
lines.text: My axe. He won it because of a squirrel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A squirrel?

lines.speaker: Yadech Strongarm
lines.text: He bet me that I couldn't catch the squirrel while still holding my axe, but that he could... Thing is, he tricked me! When it was his turn, he just chopped the tree down!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. That doesn't explain why you have a tree branch though.

lines.speaker: Yadech Strongarm
lines.text: A barbarian warrior is bound to his axe. I cannot take up another, so I use part of the tree that Sloane cut down as my weapon... It also reminds me of him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He was a great warrior.

lines.speaker: Yadech Strongarm
lines.text: Yes, and a good friend. Still, he would want us to be happy that he is now with his warrior brethren. Can I help you with anything else?

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About Lucien...

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: He will be made to pay, my friend, and I assure you, when the day of his reckoning comes, I will be there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So will I, Mazchna.

lines.speaker: Mazchna
lines.text: Then we will fight, side by side, my friend. We will fight in honour of Turael and Duradel, and we will see them avenged!

lines.speaker: Kuradal
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About Duradel...

lines.speaker: Kuradal
lines.text: Are you here with news on Lucien?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not.

lines.speaker: Kuradal
lines.text: The moment you find him, you must come to me. I will ensure my father is avenged!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He wouldn't want you to get yourself killed as well!

lines.speaker: Kuradal
lines.text: I will not suffer the same fate as my father. I intend to be prepared. For a start, I have been researching my father's notes to find weaknesses in Lucien's demonic thralls.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Those sound interesting. May I see them?

lines.speaker: Kuradal
lines.text: Of course. If we expect to defeat Lucien, we must work together.

lines.speaker: Kuradal
lines.text: Return to me when you have space to carry them.

lines.speaker: Kuradal
lines.text: I will not suffer the same fate as my father. That fate is reserved for Lucien!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hope you're right...

lines.speaker: Kuradal
lines.text: I will not suffer the same fate as my father. I intend to be prepared. For a start, I have been researching my father's notes to find weaknesses in Lucien's demonic thralls.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Speaking of those notes, I seem to have misplaced them. Do you happen to have another copy?

lines.speaker: Kuradal
lines.text: Luckily for you, I made many.

lines.speaker: Kuradal
lines.text: Return to me when you have space to carry them.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Hello there. What do you want to talk about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Hello there. I hear that Cyrisus is no longer with us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He died fighting for a good cause. He'll be remembered as a great hero.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: I suppose the good do die young.

sections.title: Attempting to contact Cyrisus via NPC Contact
